title: Shiv Chalisa
intro: Read Shri Shiv Chalisa to seek Lord Shiva's blessings. Shri Shiv Chalisa - Hymn to Lord Shiva
description:
॥ Shri Shiv Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Shri Ganesh Girija Suvan, Mangal Mool Sujan.
Kahat Ayodhyadas Tum, Dehu Abhay Vardaan.||

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jay Girija Pati Deen Dayala.
Sada Karat Santan Pratipala.
Bhaal Chandrama Sohat Neeke.
Kaanan Kundal Naagphani Ke.
Ang Gaur Shir Gang Bahaye.
Mundmaal Tan Kshaar Lagaye.
Vastra Khaal Baaghambar Sohe.
Chhavi Ko Dekhi Naag Man Mohe.
Maina Maatu Ki Have Dulari.
Baam Ang Sohat Chhavi Nyari.
Kar Trishul Sohat Chhavi Bhari.
Karat Sada Shatrun Kshaykari.
Nandi Ganesh Sohai Tahan Kaise.
Saagar Madhya Kamal Hain Jaise.
Kartik Shyam Aur Ganraau.
Ya Chhavi Ko Kahi Jaat Na Kaau.
Devan Jabahin Jaay Pukaara.
Tab Hi Dukh Prabhu Aap Nivaara.
Kiya Updrav Taarak Bhari.
Devan Sab Mili Tumhin Juhaari.
Turat Shadaanan Aap Pathaayau.
Lavnimesh Mahan Maari Giraayau.
Aap Jalandhar Asur Sanhaara.
Suyash Tumhaar Vidit Sansaara.
Tripurasur San Yuddh Machaai.
Sabahin Kripa Kar Leen Bachaai.
Kiya Tapahin Bhagirath Bhari.
Purab Pratigya Taasu Puraari.
Daanin Mahan Tum Sam Kou Naahin.
Sevak Stuti Karat Sadaahin.
Ved Naam Mahima Tav Gaai.
Akath Anaadi Bhed Nahi Paai.
Prakati Udadhi Manthan Mein Jwala.
Jarat Surasur Bhaye Vihaala.
Keenhi Daya Tahan Kari Sahaai.
Neelkanth Tab Naam Kahaai.
Poojan Ramchandra Jab Keenha.
Jeet Ke Lank Vibhishan Deenha.
Sahas Kamal Mein Ho Rahe Dhaari.
Keenha Pariksha Tabahin Puraari.
Ek Kamal Prabhu Raakheu Joi.
Kamal Nayan Poojan Chahan Soi.
Kathin Bhakti Dekhi Prabhu Shankar.
Bhaye Prasann Deeye Ichhit Var.
Jay Jay Jay Anant Avinaashi.
Karat Kripa Sab Ke Ghatvaasi.
Dusht Sakal Nit Mohi Satave.
Bhramat Rahaun Mohi Chain Na Aave.
Traahi Traahi Main Naath Pukaaro.
Yehi Avsar Mohi Aan Ubaaro.
Lai Trishul Shatrun Ko Maaro.
Sankat Se Mohi Aan Ubaaro.
Maat-Pita Bhraata Sab Hoi.
Sankat Mein Poochhat Nahi Koi.
Swami Ek Hai Aas Tumhari.
Aay Harahu Mam Sankat Bhari.
Dhan Nirdhan Ko Det Sada Hin.
Jo Koi Jaanche So Phal Paahin.
Astuti Kehi Vidhi Karain Tumhari.
Kshamahu Naath Ab Chook Hamari.
Shankar Ho Sankat Ke Naashan.
Mangal Kaaran Vighn Vinaashan.
Yogi Yati Muni Dhyan Lagaavain.
Sharad Naarad Sheesh Navaavain.
Namo Namo Jay Namah Shivay.
Sur Brahmadik Paar Na Paay.
Jo Yeh Paath Kare Man Laai.
Ta Par Hot Hai Shambhu Sahaai.
Riniyan Jo Koi Ho Adhikari.
Paath Kare So Paavan Haari.
Putra Heen Kar Ichha Joi.
Nishchay Shiv Prasad Tehi Hoi.
Pandit Trayodashi Ko Laave.
Dhyan Poorvak Hom Karaave.
Trayodashi Vrat Karai Hamesha.
Taake Tan Nahin Rahai Kalesha.
Dhoop Deep Naivedya Chadhaave.
Shankar Sammukh Paath Sunaave.
Janm Janm Ke Paap Nashaave.
Ant Dhaam Shivpur Mein Paave.
Kahain Ayodhyadas Aas Tumhari.
Jaani Sakal Dukh Harahu Hamari.

॥ Doha ॥
Nitt Nem Kar Praatah Hi, Paath Karaun Chalisa.
Tum Meri Manokamna, Purn Karo Jagdeesh.||
Magsar Chhathi Hemant Ritu, Samvat Chausath Jaan.
Astuti Chalisa Shivahi, Purn Keen Kalyan.||

faq.a1: It is a 40-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Lord Shiva.
faq.a2: Recitation brings mental peace, removes obstacles, and destroys sins.
faq.a3: Mondays, early morning, or during Pradosh Kaal are best.
faq.a4: Yes, sincere recitation creates a positive shield.
faq.a5: It is traditionally attributed to Ayodhyadas.
faq.a6: Anyone with a pure heart can recite it.
faq.a7: You can recite 1, 3, or 11 times based on your devotion.
faq.a8: No, but fasting on Mondays makes it more auspicious.
faq.a9: It is a holy day dedicated to Shiva, ideal for worship.
faq.a10: Yes, listening with full attention is equally beneficial.
